sections.title: What is a Set?
points: A set is a collection of objects that belong together.
Each object inside a set is called a member or element.
Example: { 1, 2, 3 } is a set of three numbers.

sections.title: Finite Sets
points: A finite set has a definite number of members.
You can count them and reach an end.
Example: { a, e, i, o, u } has 5 members.

sections.title: Infinite Sets
points: An infinite set goes on forever and never ends.
We write '...' to show the set continues forever.

sections.title: Counting Members
points: The number of members in a set is written as n(A).
For an infinite set, we cannot give a final count.

sections.text: Remember: A set is not infinite just because it has many members. Infinite means it never ends, like counting all natural numbers.

cards.mnemonic: Think of a set as a basket holding items.

cards.fact: A set with a fixed number of members.
n(A) shows the number of members in set A.
